parameters.content:
## Daily Study Planner — GPT-4o-mini + Google Calendar + Google Sheets + Gmail

For students who want to automatically generate a personalised week-wise study schedule from their exam date, subjects, and daily available hours — with every study session added to Google Calendar and a full tracker saved to Google Sheets. Submit the form with your name, exam date, subjects, daily hours, and email. GPT-4o-mini generates a complete day-by-day study plan with specific topics per subject, session types (New Learning, Revision, Practice), duration, and start/end times — distributed intelligently across the available days with more revision closer to the exam. A Code node parses every individual study session into flat items. SplitInBatches processes one session at a time, creating a Google Calendar event for each. Once all events are created, a Code node rebuilds the full schedule rows from the parsed data. Google Sheets appends one row per study session to the Study Planner sheet. Gmail sends a formatted HTML confirmation email with the full week-wise schedule and study tips.

## How it works
- **1. Form — Study Planner Input** collects name, exam date, subjects, daily available hours, and email
- **2. AI Agent — Generate Study Schedule** uses GPT-4o-mini to produce a complete day-by-day JSON study plan with topics, durations, and times
- **OpenAI — GPT-4o-mini Model** language model attached to the AI Agent
- **3. Code — Parse Schedule** strips markdown fences, parses JSON, flattens all daily sessions into individual items, and stores full metadata
- **4. SplitInBatches — One Session at a Time** processes each study session individually in the loop
- **5. Google Calendar — Create Study Event** creates one calendar event per study session with subject, topic, time, and session type in description
- **6. Code — Build Sheet Rows and Email** runs after all calendar events are created — rebuilds session rows from stored data and builds the HTML confirmation email
- **7. Google Sheets — Log Study Plan** appends one row per study session: date, subject, topic, duration, start/end time, session type, priority
- **8. Gmail — Send Confirmation Email** sends the full formatted HTML study schedule with tips to the student

## Set up steps
1. In **OpenAI — GPT-4o-mini Model** — connect your OpenAI API credential
2. In **5. Google Calendar — Create Study Event** — connect Google Calendar OAuth2 credential and replace `YOUR_CALENDAR_ID` (usually your email address or find it in Google Calendar settings)
3. In **7. Google Sheets — Log Study Plan** — connect Google Sheets OAuth2 credential and replace `YOUR_GOOGLE_SHEET_ID`. Create a sheet tab named Study Planner with columns: Student Name, Exam Date, Subject, Topic, Date, Day, Start Time, End Time, Duration (hrs), Session Type, Priority, Status
4. In **8. Gmail — Send Confirmation Email** — connect Gmail OAuth2 credential

nodes.name: Section — Form Input and GPT-4o-mini Schedule Generation

parameters.content:
## Form Input and GPT-4o-mini Schedule Generation
Form collects exam details. GPT-4o-mini generates a complete day-by-day study plan in strict JSON — topics per subject, session types, durations, and start/end times distributed intelligently across available days.

nodes.name: Section — Schedule Parse and Session Split

parameters.content:
## Schedule Parse and Session Split
Code strips markdown, parses JSON, and flattens all sessions into individual items. SplitInBatches loops one session at a time — output[0] processes each session, output[1] fires when all sessions are done.

nodes.name: Section — Google Calendar Event Creation Loop

parameters.content:
## Google Calendar Event Creation Loop
Creates one calendar event per study session. Loops back to SplitInBatches after each event until all sessions are created.

nodes.name: Section — Sheet Row Build, Sheets Log, and Gmail Confirmation

parameters.content:
## Sheet Row Build, Sheets Log, and Gmail Confirmation
After all calendar events are created, Code rebuilds the full session list from stored parse data. Sheets appends one row per session. Gmail sends the HTML schedule with tips.

parameters.formDescription: Enter your exam details and get a personalised week-wise study schedule added to your Google Calendar — automatically.

options.systemMessage:
You are an expert study planner and educational coach. Create a personalised week-wise study schedule for a student.

Rules:
1. Count the exact number of days from today to the exam date (inclusive of today, exclusive of exam day)
2. Distribute subjects evenly across available days — more revision sessions closer to the exam
3. First 60% of days: New Learning sessions. Last 40% of days: Revision + Practice sessions
4. Keep each session duration between 1.5 and 3 hours
5. Total sessions per day must not exceed the daily available hours
6. Spread subjects fairly — no subject should be neglected
7. Include short breaks implicitly (do not schedule break blocks)
8. Assign specific topics per subject (not just 'study mathematics' — be specific like 'Quadratic Equations', 'Integration by Parts', etc.)
9. Last 2 days before exam: only Revision and Quick Practice sessions

Session types allowed: New Learning, Revision, Practice Test, Quick Review
Priority levels: High, Medium, Low

Return ONLY a valid JSON object — no markdown, no backticks, no explanation:
{
  "student_name": "string",
  "exam_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "start_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "total_days": 14,
  "total_sessions": 28,
  "subjects": ["Subject1", "Subject2"],
  "overview_summary": "3-4 sentence summary of the study plan strategy",
  "study_tips": ["tip 1", "tip 2", "tip 3", "tip 4", "tip 5"],
  "daily_plan": [
    {
      "date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
      "day_number": 1,
      "day_of_week": "Monday",
      "sessions": [
        {
          "subject": "Mathematics",
          "topic": "Quadratic Equations — Discriminant and Roots",
          "duration_hours": 2,
          "start_time": "09:00",
          "end_time": "11:00",
          "session_type": "New Learning",
          "priority": "High"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

Return ONLY the JSON object. Nothing before or after it.

nodes.name: 4. SplitInBatches — One Session at a Time
6. Code — Build Sheet Rows and Email
